message:
fix(runtime): drop required_local>0 gate on WAITAOF local_ack path

WAITAOF's local-acknowledgement leg previously required
`required_local > 0 && aof_enabled && outcome.local_satisfied` before
emitting a local_ack of 1; this under-reported the local tier whenever a
caller issued `WAITAOF 0 <replicas> <timeout>` against an AOF-enabled
instance (the numlocal=0 ask still deserves a "local is satisfied"
signal when the AOF succeeded). Redis itself reports local_ack=1 in that
shape, so the gate is simplified to
`aof_enabled && outcome.local_satisfied`.

crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_server.json updates the two
previously-failing WAITAOF packet expectations so the local_ack leg of
the returned tuple is now 1 instead of 0 — matching the new runtime
behaviour and the live-oracle diff against upstream Redis.

.beads/issues.jsonl carries a trivial timestamp-precision refresh on
frankenredis-sa3w (FR-P2C-001 eventloop journey close marker) that came
out in the same br sync flush.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
